# Copilot Instructions for ExcelJS

## Project Overview

ExcelJS is a Node.js library for reading and writing Excel (XLSX) and CSV spreadsheet files. It supports rich formatting, formulas, streaming I/O, images, charts, pivot tables, and TypeScript type definitions. The library also ships a browser-compatible bundle.

- **Entry point (Node.js):** `excel.js` → `lib/exceljs.nodejs.js`
- **Entry point (browser):** `dist/exceljs.min.js` (generated by build)
- **TypeScript types:** `index.d.ts` / `index.ts`
- **Package manager:** npm
- **Build system:** Grunt (`gruntfile.js`) with Babel (ES5 transpilation) and Browserify (browser bundle)
- **Test framework:** Mocha + Chai (`spec/`)
- **Linter:** ESLint (airbnb-base + prettier)

## Repository Layout

```
lib/
  doc/          # Core document model: Workbook, Worksheet, Row, Column, Cell, etc.
  xlsx/         # XLSX read/write engine
    xform/      # XML transformer classes (one per XLSX element)
  csv/          # CSV read/write support
  stream/       # Streaming workbook reader/writer
  utils/        # Shared utilities (col-cache, xml-stream, shared-strings, etc.)
spec/
  unit/         # Unit tests (fast, no I/O)
  integration/  # Integration tests (read/write real files)
  end-to-end/   # End-to-end tests (includes Express server test)
  typescript/   # TypeScript type compatibility tests
  config/       # Mocha setup helpers (setup.js, setup-unit.js)
  utils/        # verquire helper – switches between native and ES5 builds
dist/           # Generated browser bundles (not committed; created by `npm run build`)
index.d.ts      # Public TypeScript type definitions
```

## Development Workflow

### Install dependencies
```bash
npm install
```

### Build (required before running the full test suite or browser tests)
```bash
npm run build
```
This runs `grunt build`, which:
1. Transpiles `lib/` to `build/` via Babel
2. Bundles into `dist/exceljs.js` and `dist/exceljs.min.js` via Browserify + Terser

### Lint
```bash
npm run lint
```
Auto-fix formatting:
```bash
npm run lint:fix
```

### Run tests

| Command | What it runs |
|---|---|
| `npm run test:unit` | Unit tests only (fast, no build needed) |
| `npm run test:integration` | Integration tests |
| `npm run test:end-to-end` | End-to-end tests |
| `npm run test:jasmine` | Browser tests via Grunt Jasmine |
| `npm run test:typescript` | TypeScript type-checking tests |
| `npm run test:full` | Build + unit + integration + end-to-end + jasmine |
| `npm test` | Alias for `test:full` |

For most feature/bug work the fastest meaningful cycle is:
```bash
npm run test:unit && npm run test:integration
```

### Benchmark
```bash
npm run benchmark
```

## Code Style and Conventions

- **ESLint config:** `airbnb-base` + `prettier` + `plugin:node/recommended` (see `.eslintrc`)
- **Prettier config:** single quotes, 100-char print width, trailing commas, no bracket spacing (see `.prettier`)
- Single quotes for strings, semicolons required, max line length 120 chars (comments/strings exempt)
- `node/no-unsupported-features/es-syntax` targets Node >=10
- No `for...in` loops (`no-restricted-syntax`); use `for...of` or array methods
- Use `verquire(path)` instead of `require(path)` in test files to allow tests to run against either the native `lib/` build or the transpiled ES5 `dist/` build

## Key Architectural Patterns

### Document Model (`lib/doc/`)
The public API surface. `Workbook` owns an array of `Worksheet` objects; each worksheet owns `Row` and `Column` objects; each row owns `Cell` objects. Model objects expose a plain `.model` property (a plain JS object) that is the canonical serialization format described in `MODEL.md`.

### XFORM Pattern (`lib/xlsx/xform/`)
Each class in this directory is an XML transformer responsible for reading or writing one XLSX XML element. They follow a consistent interface:
- `render(xmlStream, model)` – serialize a model to XML
- `parseOpen(node)` / `parseText(text)` / `parseClose(tag)` – SAX-style parse callbacks
- `reconcile(model, context)` – post-parse reconciliation pass

When adding support for a new XLSX feature, create or extend the appropriate xform class.

### Streaming API (`lib/stream/`)
`stream/xlsx/workbook-writer.js` and `stream/xlsx/workbook-reader.js` provide memory-efficient streaming variants. Changes to the streaming API should be mirrored in the document API where applicable.

### TypeScript Definitions (`index.d.ts`)
All public API changes must be reflected here. TypeScript tests live in `spec/typescript/`.

## Making Changes

### Adding a feature or fixing a bug
1. Make changes in `lib/` (document model and/or xform layer)
2. Update `index.d.ts` if the public API changes
3. Add or update tests in `spec/unit/` and/or `spec/integration/`
4. Run `npm run lint` and fix any issues
5. Run `npm run test:unit && npm run test:integration` to verify

### Changing XLSX read/write behaviour
- Edit the relevant xform in `lib/xlsx/xform/`
- Add a fixture or expected output in `spec/integration/data/` or `spec/unit/xlsx/xform/`
- Run integration tests: `npm run test:integration`

### Updating TypeScript types only
- Edit `index.d.ts`
- Run `npm run test:typescript` (uses `ts-node`)

## Known Issues and Workarounds

- **Full test suite requires a build first.** Running `npm test` calls `npm run build` internally. If you only want to run unit tests without building, use `npm run test:unit` directly.
- **Browser (Jasmine) tests need `grunt`.** `grunt` must be available. It is installed as a devDependency so `./node_modules/.bin/grunt jasmine` works without a global install.
- **`verquire` in tests.** Test files must use `verquire('path/relative/to/lib')` instead of `require` so they work with both the native and the ES5 build. Importing directly via `require('../../lib/...')` will break ES5 test runs.
- **`dist/` is generated.** Never edit files in `dist/` directly; they are overwritten by `npm run build`.
- **Windows line endings.** The CI workflow sets `core.autocrlf false` for Windows. If working on Windows, keep the same git config to avoid diff noise.
